title: Lushier Forests
description: Staying true to vanilla's style whilst giving more life to Mineraft's nature - through variations in models and textures
body:
## About

You're fed up about Minecraft's boring Nature? To you I present a pack which aims to improve this issue! I aim to maintain the vanilla-Minecraft feel and lean on concept arts, whilst drastically improving it. My pack uses many variated textures and 3D-models to immerse you into the game.

Its still in really early alpha, so don't expect all biomes and woods to be covered. Though i do want to someday improve every part of the game's nature!

## A small preview

![This is what birch forests look like!](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/a6f210d828d759c54136b6fdc5c1819a528b33c6.jpeg)

## Installation

Minimal installation required! Just download the pack and enjoy. Optionally, also install the Continuity Mod, which adds a few extra things (eg. log roots).

## Weird dark grass when using shaders?


<details>
<summary>Spoiler</summary>

![See how the grass casts a very akward shadow on the left?](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/44d05cd4d81772b1a3ca6512093b2bb2b01b622a.jpeg)

This is a result of the shaderpack's lack of Subsurface-Scattering on grass blocks. Fortunately, this can be fixed quickly and easily!
![Using 7Zip to edit the block properties in a shaderpack](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/aed605932afd9076926f42c239541654608c3a45.png)
Go into the shaderpack folder/zip and edit the block.properties in the shaders folder to include "grass_block" under e.g. SSS or in any group with sugarcane, vines, etc.

Sadly i dont really know of a solution in my resource pack itself, but this seems to be the best fix.


</details>


## Extras

It is an original pack by me (SussyBauarbeiter). I appreciate anyone using this pack as inspiration/incorporating it in your packs - when credit to original code is given - but please don't directly steal :)
